FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-28-2009 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

A Citation will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to show cause as to why the Trustee's powers should not be suspended, and as to why they should not be surcharged for failure to appear and for failure to file the Third Account. The return date is February 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (305 Juanita Way, San Francisoc, Ca)
Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Kathy Nakagawa.

The sale was confirmed at $835,000.00. An additional bond in the amount of $835,000.00 is required. The request to close the sale in 40 days was approved by the Court. The Court granted the petition (subject to a written order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Sara Lerschen, CSR#6213. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. McDaniels.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jun-24-2009 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Continued To Jun-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Kathy Nakagawa (Trustee).

Ms. Navia explained that the Trust had two pieces of properties, that the multi unit had been sold, that they were in the process of selling the second property, that she hoped it would sell by the end of the month and that she wanted to wait to file the final accounting to include the proceeds from both properties. The Court directed Ms. Navia to file the Second Accounting which is to cover from April 1, 2006 through March 31, 2008. The matter was continued to June 24, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the Second Account.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Continued To May-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1025 Stanyan Street, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Dec-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Kathy Nakagawa (Successor Trustee).

The property sold for $1,978,168.00. The Court confirmed the sale of 50% of the property belonging to the trust in the amount of $989,084.00 (sigining the order for the same). An additional bond in the amount of $309,000.00 is required.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Nov-28-2006 Continued To May-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia (Counsel for Kathy Nakagawa, Trustee); Kathy Nakagawa (Trustee).

As Ms. Navia had not had the opportunity to review the most recent Examiner's notes, the Court reported the same, addressing the primary concern that the real property was not income producing. Ms. Navia and Ms. Nakagawa both reported on the difficulty of Mr. Lee's involvement (or want thereof), including his suspect health. The Court indicated its interest in seeing some activity with respect to the property - either by sale or rent - and continued the matter until May 29, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. to allow the Trustee time to address these concerns and better determine the status of Mr. Lee's health.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer CSR# 5802; Bailiff: Deputies Cruz and Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnsen.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Nov-14-2006 Continued To Nov-28-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

First And Final Account And Rpt Of Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-26-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Called with case number 286881. Rowena Navia appearing for and with Kathy Nakagawa. Court will allow the accounting because of the transfer of funds from the coservatorship to the trust without court order. No further transfers are allowed without court order. The conservatee wants to come to court today but she is ill. She wants to sell the real property. Court will review the order before signing it. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton, CSR #1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
